heading: Ballistic missile defense programs: program elements
label: 5511 Ballistic missile defense programs: program elements
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to repeal by , , .section 223 of this titlePub. L. 118–159, div. A, title XVI, § 1649(b)(1)138 Stat. 2199Dec. 23, 2024

Budget and Acquisition Requirements for Missile Defense Agency Activities, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–181, div. A, title II, § 223122 Stat. 39Pub. L. 112–81, div. A, title II, § 231(b)(2)125 Stat. 1339Jan. 28, 2008Dec. 31, 2011“(a).—Revised Budget StructureThe budget justification materials submitted to Congress in support of the Department of Defense budget for any fiscal year after fiscal year 2009 (as submitted with the budget of the President under , United States Code) shall set forth separately amounts requested for the Missile Defense Agency for each of the following:section 1105(a) of title 31“(1)Research, development, test, and evaluation.“(2)Procurement.“(3)Operation and maintenance.“(4)Military construction.“(b)2009.—Revised Budget Structure for Fiscal YearThe budget justification materials submitted to Congress in support of the Department of Defense budget for fiscal year 2009 (as submitted with the budget of the President under , United States Code) shall—section 1105(a) of title 31“(1)identify all known and estimated operation and support costs; and“(2)set forth separately amounts requested for the Missile Defense Agency for each of the following:“(A)Research, development, test, and evaluation.“(B)Procurement or advance procurement of long lead items, including for Terminal High Altitude Area Defense firing units 3 and 4, and for Standard Missile-3 Block 1A interceptors.“(C)Military construction.“(c)2009.—Availability of RDT&E Funds for Fiscal YearUpon approval by the Secretary of Defense, and consistent with the plan submitted under subsection (f), funds appropriated pursuant to an authorization of appropriations or otherwise made available for fiscal year 2009 for research, development, test, and evaluation for the Missile Defense Agency—“(1)may be used for the fielding of ballistic missile defense capabilities approved previously by Congress; and“(2)may not be used for—“(A)military construction activities; or“(B)procurement or advance procurement of long lead items, including for Terminal High Altitude Area Defense firing units 3 and 4, and for Standard Missile-3 Block 1A interceptors.“(d)2009 2010.—Full Funding Requirement Not Applicable to Use of Procurement Funds for Fiscal YearsandIn any case in which funds appropriated pursuant to an authorization of appropriations or otherwise made available for procurement for the Missile Defense Agency for fiscal years 2009 and 2010 are used for the fielding of ballistic missile defense capabilities, the funds may be used for the fielding of those capabilities on an ‘incremental’ basis, notwithstanding any law or policy of the Department of Defense that would otherwise require a ‘full funding’ basis.“(e).—Relationship to Other LawNothing in this provision shall be construed to alter or otherwise affect in any way the applicability of the requirements and other provisions of section 234(a) through (d) of the Ronald W. Reagan National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2005 (; ; [former] note).Public Law 108–375118 Stat. 183710 U.S.C. 2431“(f).—Plan RequiredNot later than , the Director of the Missile Defense Agency shall submit to the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives a plan for transitioning the Missile Defense Agency from using exclusively research, development, test, and evaluation funds to using procurement, military construction, operations and maintenance, and research, development, test, and evaluation funds for the appropriate budget activities, and for transitioning from incremental funding to full funding for fiscal years after fiscal year 2010.”March 1, 2008

References to New Name for Ballistic Missile Defense Organization, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–314, div. A, title II, § 225(a)116 Stat. 2486Dec. 2, 2002“Any reference to the Ballistic Missile Defense Organization in any provision of law, regulation, map, document, record, or other paper of the United States shall be considered to be a reference to the Missile Defense Agency.”

children.heading: .—Program Elements Specified by President
children.label: a .—Program Elements Specified by President
children.text: In the budget justification materials submitted to Congress in support of the Department of Defense budget for any fiscal year (as submitted with the budget of the President under ), the amount requested for activities of the Missile Defense Agency shall be set forth in accordance with such program elements as the President may specify.section 1105(a) of title 31

children.heading: .—Separate Program Elements for Programs Entering Engineering and Manufacturing Development
children.label: b .—Separate Program Elements for Programs Entering Engineering and Manufacturing Development

children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that each ballistic missile defense program that enters engineering and manufacturing development is assigned a separate, dedicated program element.

children.label: A
children.text: translate the most promising design approach into a stable, interoperable, producible, supportable, and cost-effective design;

children.label: B
children.text: validate the manufacturing or production process; and

children.heading: .—Management and Support
children.label: c .—Management and Support
children.text: The amount requested for a fiscal year for any program element specified for that fiscal year pursuant to subsection (a) shall include requests for the amounts necessary for the management and support of the programs, projects, and activities contained in that program element.
